danger_signals.visual_form: balloon disappears from player character helmet; character model loses visual asset
danger_signals.meaning: loss of flotation ability; increased vulnerability; one step closer to death

danger_signals.visual_form: blue horizontal band at bottom of screen representing water
danger_signals.meaning: instant death if player falls into it; environmental hazard

danger_signals.visual_form: large animated fish sprite near water surface
danger_signals.meaning: predatory hazard; instant death if player flies too close to water

danger_signals.visual_form: animated electrical effects moving across screen in Balloon Trip mode
danger_signals.meaning: collision hazard; causes instant death in Balloon Trip mode

danger_signals.visual_form: identical character sprite to player but controlled by AI; floats around screen
danger_signals.meaning: active threat; will attempt to pop player balloons; must be defeated by popping enemy balloons first

safety_signals.visual_form: two balloon shapes attached to player character helmet
safety_signals.meaning: player is alive and has full flotation; protection against one balloon-pop

safety_signals.visual_form: solid horizontal surfaces scattered throughout screen; varied architectural elements
safety_signals.meaning: safe resting points; allow player to pause and plan; prevent immediate water hazard

safety_signals.visual_form: all enemy sprites removed from play area
safety_signals.meaning: level victory; progression to next stage; temporary safety state

safety_signals.visual_form: animated bubble sprite floating upward after enemy defeat
safety_signals.meaning: collectible reward; bonus points; visual feedback of successful enemy elimination

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: balloon helmet with two inflated balloons
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: enemy Balloon Fighters appear visually identical but are AI-controlled opponents
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: player character always centered or player-controlled; spawns at level start; always has helmet balloons as status indicator

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: balloon helmet with two inflated balloons (identical to player)
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: distinguished only by AI movement patterns and screen position; no visual differentiation from player model
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: multiple copies spawn on screen; float autonomously; balloons are identical but must be popped by player action

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: enemies present on screen; platforms visible; water hazard at bottom edge
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: enemies float around autonomously; player must engage in combat; platforms anchor gameplay geography

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: all enemy sprites removed; screen returns to empty state with platforms only
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: stage progression triggered; player advances to next level; bonus stage may appear every three stages

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: balloons float up from chimneys at bottom of screen; no enemy fighters present
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: player switches to collection mode; goal becomes hitting balloons for points rather than defeating enemies

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: side-scrolling perspective; lightning effects animate across screen; single life indicator shown
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: obstacle avoidance replaces enemy combat; high-score focus; continuous horizontal movement; one life only

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: single balloon visible on player character helmet (second balloon missing/popped)
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: critical vulnerability state; next balloon pop triggers death; increased difficulty perception

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: both balloons popped; player character disappears or collapses; life counter decrements
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: game over or respawn depending on remaining lives; level may restart or fail

environmental_storytelling.example: water at screen bottom establishes the playing field as a contained vertical space above a deadly boundary; tells player they must stay airborne

environmental_storytelling.visual_example: piranha fish sprite near water surface suggests aquatic danger; teaches player water = death without explicit text

environmental_storytelling.example: scattered platforms of varying heights and positions communicate level difficulty progression; stages add more platforms suggesting safer complexity later in game
enemy Balloon Fighters appear identical to player character, establishing competitive parity; no visual hierarchy suggests fair fight mechanic
chimneys spouting balloons in bonus stages visually communicate resource/point generation; architectural element signals game mode shift

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: flaps arms repeatedly; rises vertically when A button pressed or B button held; floats upward with momentum
falls downward when no input given; accelerates toward water if balloons are popped; reduced buoyancy visually apparent
slower vertical rise rate; struggles to maintain altitude; visual indicator of compromised state
floats around screen attempting to maneuver toward player position; responds to player proximity; autonomous movement pattern
disappears from screen; replaced by rising bubble sprite; no death animation present
positioned at or near water surface; remains stationary or swims horizontally; threatens any player flying too low
animated electrical effect sweeping across screen in Balloon Trip mode; deterministic pattern; unavoidable except through careful positioning

notes: Balloon Fight uses minimalist visual language typical of 1985 NES design. No faction complexity exists—conflict is purely player versus identical-looking enemies with no political or social dimension. World state communication relies entirely on sprite presence/absence, balloon count, and hazard positioning. The game establishes danger through simple visual rules: two balloons = safe, one = critical, zero = dead; water = instant death; lightning in trip mode = instant death. Environmental storytelling is implicit in geometry (platforms) and sprite placement (fish, enemies, chimneys). No dialogue, text, or symbolic communication of world state beyond visual gameplay elements.
